zotero.abstractNote: We propose Spatially Structured Recurrent Modules (S2RMs), a novel architecture for processing visual information. S2RMs are composed of a set of recurrent modules that operate on local patches of an image. These modules are spatially arranged in a grid and communicate with their neighbors, allowing the model to learn spatial dependencies and capture long-range interactions. We demonstrate that S2RMs achieve state-of-the-art performance on several visual reasoning tasks and exhibit strong generalization capabilities to unseen spatial configurations.
